questions.q: Devadatta was the battle conch of which hero?
questions.explanation: Devadatta was Arjuna's celebrated conch, sounded on every field he fought.

questions.q: What does the name Devadatta mean?
questions.explanation: Devadatta means god-given, fitting a conch of celestial origin.

questions.q: What colour is Arjuna's conch Devadatta traditionally described as?
questions.explanation: Devadatta is pictured as a great white conch, like the other divine shells of the war.

questions.q: What effect did the blast of Devadatta have on Arjuna's enemies?
options: It deafened their horses and elephants
It drained the courage from their hearts
questions.explanation: The conch's note was said to unman enemy hearts, spreading dread through hostile ranks.

questions.q: Which conch did Krishna sound beside Devadatta at the opening of the war?
questions.explanation: Krishna blew Panchajanya and Arjuna answered with Devadatta as the war began.

questions.q: At the opening of which battle did Arjuna famously blow Devadatta beside Krishna?
questions.explanation: The joint blast of Panchajanya and Devadatta announced the start of the Kurukshetra war.

questions.q: The sounding of Devadatta and the other conches opens the first chapter of which text?
questions.explanation: The first chapter of the Bhagavad Gita names each warrior's conch as the armies face each other.

questions.q: Each Pandava sounded a named conch at the war's start; which one was Bhima's?
questions.explanation: Bhima, the wolf-bellied, blew the conch called Paundra.

questions.q: Which conch belonged to Yudhishthira?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira sounded Anantavijaya, the conch of endless victory.

questions.q: How many Pandava brothers blew their own named conches at Kurukshetra's opening?
questions.explanation: All five brothers sounded their named shells, Devadatta being one voice among the five.

questions.q: Which conch did Nakula sound at the war's opening?
questions.explanation: Nakula blew Sughosha, the sweet-sounding conch.

questions.q: Which conch did Sahadeva sound at the war's opening?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva's conch was Manipushpaka, the jewel-flowered.

questions.q: On the Kaurava side, who blew his conch first, setting off the general clamour before Devadatta answered?
questions.explanation: Bhishma raised a lion-roar and blew his conch to hearten Duryodhana, and the Kaurava host erupted after him.

questions.q: According to the Gita, the roar of the Pandava conches tore at whose hearts?
options: The hearts of the watching gods
The hearts of the Pandava host
The hearts of the beasts of war
questions.explanation: The Gita says the tumult rent the hearts of the sons of Dhritarashtra as it echoed over the field.

questions.q: According to the Sabha Parva, who presented the conch Devadatta to Arjuna?
questions.explanation: Maya gave Arjuna the conch while building the great hall, though some retellings credit Indra with the gift.

questions.q: From where did Maya fetch the conch he gave to Arjuna?
options: The depths of the western ocean
The lake Bindusaras in the north
questions.explanation: Maya retrieved his stored treasures from Lake Bindusaras near Kailasa, the conch Devadatta among them.

questions.q: What did Maya bring for Bhima at the same time as Arjuna's conch?
questions.explanation: From the same hoard Maya presented Bhima with a great club worthy of his strength.

questions.q: When Arjuna blew Devadatta at Kurukshetra, who roared in answer from his chariot banner?
questions.explanation: Hanuman sat on the Kapi Dhwaja banner and added his roar to the conch's blast, terrifying the enemy.

questions.q: Where were Krishna and Arjuna when they sounded their conches in the Gita's opening scene?
options: In a great chariot yoked to white horses
On a rise before the Pandava camp
Between the river and the western ridge
At the head of the elephant division
questions.explanation: The Gita places them stationed in their great chariot drawn by white steeds as they blew the divine shells.

questions.q: During the year in disguise, in which fight did Devadatta's blast help announce that Arjuna had returned to the field?
options: The duel with Kichaka in the dance hall
The cattle raid against the Kurus at Virata
questions.explanation: When the Kurus raided Virata's cattle, Arjuna cast off his disguise, and his conch and bow announced him.

questions.q: In the Gita's verse naming the two conches, by what epithet is Arjuna called?
questions.explanation: The verse says Dhananjaya, the winner of wealth, blew Devadatta.

questions.q: What did the combined blast of the conches do, in the Gita's description?
options: It filled sky and earth with its echo
It silenced both armies for an hour
It startled the sun behind clouds
questions.explanation: The tumultuous uproar is said to have resounded through both heaven and earth.

questions.q: Which conch does the Gita's listing single out with the word mahashankha, the great conch?
questions.explanation: The Gita calls Paundra, blown by the wolf-bellied Bhima, the mahashankha or great conch.

questions.q: What followed immediately after Bhishma's lion-roar and conch blast on the Kaurava side?
options: A volley of arrows blotting the sun
A sudden uproar of conches, drums, cymbals, and horns
The advance of the eleven akshauhinis
questions.explanation: Conches, kettledrums, cymbals, and horns burst out all at once across the Kaurava host.

questions.q: Devadatta answered Krishna's Panchajanya at the war's opening; from what episode did Krishna's own conch come?
options: A boon from Rudra at Kailasa
His slaying of the sea demon Panchajana
questions.explanation: Krishna took Panchajanya from the sea demon Panchajana, whom he slew to recover his guru's son.

questions.q: Krishna had vowed to bear no arms in the war, yet he sounded his conch beside Arjuna's; why was this no breach?
options: Blowing a conch was no act of combat
The vow began only with the first arrow
He blew it standing outside the field
questions.explanation: Sounding a conch, like driving the chariot, was not fighting, so the charioteer's vow remained intact.

questions.q: In the Gita's sequence, who sounded his conch immediately after Krishna?
questions.explanation: Hrishikesha blew Panchajanya and Dhananjaya followed at once with Devadatta, before Bhima and the others.

questions.q: When Devadatta's note rang out over the cattle raid at Virata, what did the Kuru commanders conclude?
options: That Virata's full army had arrived
That Arjuna himself had taken the field
That a storm was breaking over the plain
questions.explanation: Drona and the elders knew the signs at once and declared that the warrior before them could only be Arjuna.

questions.q: In the verse pairing Panchajanya and Devadatta, by what name is Krishna called?
questions.explanation: The verse names him Hrishikesha, the lord of the senses, as he blows Panchajanya.

questions.q: What did Arjuna ask of Krishna immediately after the conches were sounded?
options: To sound the advance of the host
To draw the chariot between the two armies
To carry him first to Bhishma's feet
questions.explanation: Arjuna asked Krishna to place the chariot between the armies so he could survey those he must fight, and the Gita began.
